Effective Date of 1988 AmendmentAmendment by section 1013(a)(6)–(8) of effective, except as otherwise provided, as if included in the provision of the Tax Reform Act of 1986, , to which such amendment relates, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 100–647Pub. L. 99–514section 1019(a) of Pub. L. 100–647section 1 of this title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 100–647, title V, § 5053(c)102 Stat. 3678Nov. 10, 1988“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [amending this section and ] shall apply to obligations issued after .section 148 of this titleOctober 21, 1988“(2)Exception for construction or binding agreement.—“(A)The amendments made by this section shall not apply to bonds (other than refunding bonds) with respect to a facility—“(i)(I)the original use of which begins with the taxpayer, and the construction, reconstruction, or rehabilitation of which began before , and was completed on or after such date, orJuly 14, 1988“(II)the original use of which begins with the taxpayer and with respect to which a binding contract to incur significant expenditures for construction, reconstruction, or rehabilitation was entered into before , and some of such expenditures are incurred on or after such date, andJuly 14, 1988“(ii)described in an inducement resolution or other comparable preliminary approval adopted by an issuing authority (or by a voter referendum) before .July 14, 1988For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term ‘significant expenditures’ means expenditures greater than 10 percent of the reasonably anticipated cost of the construction, reconstruction, or rehabilitation of the facility involved.“(B)Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any bond issued after , and shall not apply unless it is reasonably expected (at the time of issuance of the bond) that the facility will be placed in service before .December 31, 1989January 1, 1990“(3).—RefundingsThe amendments made by this section shall not apply to any bond issued to refund (or which is part of a series of bonds issued to refund) a bond issued before , if—July 15, 1988“(A)the average maturity date of the issue of which the refunding bond is a part is not later than the average maturity date of the bonds to be refunded by such issue,“(B)the amount of the refunding bond does not exceed the outstanding amount of the refunded bond, and“(C)the proceeds of the refunding bond are used to redeem the refunded bond not later than 90 days after the date of the issuance of the refunding bond.For purposes of subparagraph (A), average maturity shall be determined in accordance with section 147(b) of the 1986 Code.”

children.text: all property which is to be provided by the net proceeds of the issue is to be owned by a 501(c)(3) organization or a governmental unit, and

children.label: A
children.text: 501(c)(3) organizations were treated as governmental units with respect to their activities which do not constitute unrelated trades or businesses, determined by applying section 513(a), and

children.label: B
children.text: paragraphs (1) and (2) of section 141(b) were applied by substituting “5 percent” for “10 percent” each place it appears and by substituting “net proceeds” for “proceeds” each place it appears.

children.heading: $150,000,000 limitation on bonds other than hospital bonds
children.label: b $150,000,000 limitation on bonds other than hospital bonds

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A bond (other than a qualified hospital bond) shall not be treated as a qualified 501(c)(3) bond if the aggregate authorized face amount of the issue (of which such bond is a part) allocated to any 501(c)(3) organization which is a test-period beneficiary (when increased by the outstanding tax-exempt nonhospital bonds of such organization) exceeds $150,000,000.

children.label: i
children.text: which are allocated to such organization, and

children.label: ii
children.text: which are outstanding at the time of such later issue (not including as outstanding any bond which is to be redeemed (other than in an advance refunding) from the net proceeds of the later issue).

children.label: i
children.text: any qualified 501(c)(3) bond other than a qualified hospital bond, and

children.label: I
children.text: such bond would have been an industrial development bond (as defined in section 103(b)(2), as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the Tax Reform Act of 1986) if 501(c)(3) organizations were not exempt persons, and

children.label: II
children.text: such bond was not described in paragraph (4), (5), or (6) of such section 103(b) (as in effect on the date such bond was issued).

children.heading: Only nonhospital portion of bonds taken into account
children.label: C Only nonhospital portion of bonds taken into account

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A bond shall be taken into account under subparagraph (B) only to the extent that the proceeds of the issue of which such bond is a part are not used with respect to a hospital.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: ii Special rule
children.text: If 90 percent or more of the net proceeds of an issue are used with respect to a hospital, no bond which is part of such issue shall be taken into account under subparagraph (B)(ii).

children.heading: Aggregation rule
children.label: 3 Aggregation rule
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, 2 or more organizations under common management or control shall be treated as 1 organization.

children.heading: Allocation of face amount of issue; test-period beneficiary
children.label: 4 Allocation of face amount of issue; test-period beneficiary
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subparagraphs (C), (D), and (E) of section 144(a)(10) shall apply for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Termination of limitation
children.label: 5 Termination of limitation
children.text: This subsection shall not apply with respect to bonds issued after the date of the enactment of this paragraph as part of an issue 95 percent or more of the net proceeds of which are to be used to finance capital expenditures incurred after such date.

children.heading: Qualified hospital bond
children.label: c Qualified hospital bond
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “qualified hospital bond” means any bond issued as part of an issue 95 percent or more of the net proceeds of which are to be used with respect to a hospital.

children.heading: Restrictions on bonds used to provide residential rental housing for family units
children.label: d Restrictions on bonds used to provide residential rental housing for family units

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection, a bond which is part of an issue shall not be a qualified 501(c)(3) bond if any portion of the net proceeds of the issue are to be used directly or indirectly to provide residential rental property for family units.

children.heading: Exception for bonds used to provide qualified residential rental projects
children.label: 2 Exception for bonds used to provide qualified residential rental projects
A
children.text: a residential rental property for family units if the first use of such property is pursuant to such issue,

children.label: B
children.text: qualified residential rental projects (as defined in section 142(d)), or

children.label: C
children.text: property which is to be substantially rehabilitated in a rehabilitation beginning within the 2-year period ending 1 year after the date of the acquisition of such property.

children.heading: Certain property treated as new property
children.label: 3 Certain property treated as new property
i
children.text: the 1st use of property is pursuant to taxable financing,

children.label: ii
children.text: there was a reasonable expectation (at the time such taxable financing was provided) that such financing would be replaced by tax-exempt financing, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the taxable financing is in fact so replaced within a reasonable period after the taxable financing was provided,

children.heading: Special rule where no operating State or local program for tax-exempt financing
children.label: B Special rule where no operating State or local program for tax-exempt financing
children.text: If, at the time of the 1st use of property, there was no operating State or local program for tax-exempt financing of the property, the 1st use of the property shall be treated as pursuant to the 1st tax-exempt financing of the property.

children.heading: Tax-exempt financing
children.label: i Tax-exempt financing
children.text: The term “tax-exempt financing” means financing provided by tax-exempt bonds.

children.heading: Taxable financing
children.label: ii Taxable financing
children.text: The term “taxable financing” means financing which is not tax-exempt financing.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), rules similar to the rules of section 47(c)(1)(B) shall apply in determining for purposes of paragraph (2)(C) whether property is substantially rehabilitated.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), clause (ii) of section 47(c)(1)(B) shall not apply, but the Secretary may extend the 24-month period in section 47(c)(1)(B)(i) where appropriate due to circumstances not within the control of the owner.
the issuer elects not to have this section apply to such issue, and
such issue is an issue of exempt facility bonds, or qualified redevelopment bonds, to which section 146 applies.
